programmes.show_description: Celebrating the eclectic remix catalogue of Kieran Hebden, better known as Four Tet.
6 Music takes you to the dark side with the finest goth, electronic and dark pop tracks around, featuring This Mortal Coil, Dot Allison, John Glacier, Barry Can't Swim and Wardruna.
Cillian handpicks music by Portishead, Aretha Franklin, A Tribe Called Quest, David Bowie, Low and Arthur Russell.
Icelandic post rock and ambient band Sigur Rós select two hours of absorbing ambient music to help you find your focus.
Afrodeutsche brings the mellow weekend flow you need to hit that deeply relaxing vibe.
Mark Radcliffe presents a feast of great music and singer-songwriter Tori Amos shares her 'First, Last and Everything' and chats about her new album.
Deb sets the weekend vibe with a magical multigenre selection from house to dub to hip-hop to rock to soul and beyond - including music from Sault, Jai Paul, Hole and Stevie Wonder.
Jamz speaks with London-based vocalist, composer and keys player Clara Serra López to premiere tracks from her new EP, blending Catalan and Cuban roots with jazz harmony.
Gilles invites Brazilian singer-songwriter Sessa to perform four tracks from his new album, Pequema Vertigem De Amor. Plus, words from Tara Clerkin Trio, Nubian Twist, and Lakecia.

programmes.show_name: The Craig Charles Funk and Soul Show
programmes.show_description: The Trunk of Funk comes from The Bandulus, a soul and reggae outfit led by producer and musician Jeremy Peña.
Don's Saturday night selections cross time, space and genre. This week, he's got numbers by Primal Scream, Dub Pistols, Hollie Cook, Tinlicker and Zion Train.
SHERELLE welcomes rising pop star Natanya onto the show to chat about her musical beginnings, her affinity with jazz music, and her latest project 'Feline's Return Act II'.
The London-based production duo bring their signature electro sound to the Sweatbox, with a mix of original productions and unreleased gems.
All the best rave tracks for romancing, featuring classic 2-step, garage and house. Music from Crazy P, Kele Le Roc, Motor City Drum Ensemble, Cloud 9 and more!
Breathe in this chilled and calming curated mix featuring Imogen Heap, Tears For Fears and Maribou State.
Ahead of the band's 6 Music Festival performance, Sam from Yard Act selects the tracks for this chilled mix. With music from Glen Campbell, Talk Talk, Minnie Riperton and more.
Afrodeutsche spins some sensational tunes to kick off your Sunday the right way.
Mark Radcliffe presents a feast of great music and chat for weekend breakfast.
Alt n Gün bring Turkish psych-groove from their new album 'Garip', while Jim Al-Khalili shares stories of life, science and curiosity from The Life Scientific.
Guy Garvey brings you some appropriate music for both Mother's Day and celebrating Ry Cooder's birthday.
Iggy returns with a sonic cocktail to soundtrack your Sunday afternoon. This week, he's got key cuts by the likes of Kleenex, Stereolab, The Who, Ethel Cain, Mogwai and more.
Artist Sue Webster selects her End of Days choice, the piece of music she would like to leave to future generations by Siouxsie and the Banshees, the band who shaped her youth.
Stuart's featured album is the Mardi Gras voodoo jazz swamp ritual classic Gris Gris by Dr. John the Night Tripper.
Zakia is joined in the studio by the most special guest of all - her mum, Amey, who shares some of her favourite late-night tracks as well as some motherly advice.
